title: Future Stock

jokes.text: Where am I? Move forward! Walk into the light!
jokes.setup: Professor appears confused at stockholder meeting
jokes.punchline: Stockholders treat him like he's dying and should go into the light
jokes.explanation: Classic Futurama absurdism - stockholders treating senile Professor like he's dying. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Oh, God! I'm dead! Well, no matter.
jokes.setup: Professor thinks he's dead from the 'go into light' comment
jokes.punchline: Completely casual 'well, no matter' response to being dead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment - nonchalant reaction to thinking he's dead. Excellent economy and character work.

jokes.text: Thank you all for coming. I don't recognize any of you, nor can I recall why I am here.
jokes.setup: Professor addressing stockholders in formal business setting
jokes.punchline: Admits complete confusion about who they are and why he's there
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Professor's senility, creates awkward business meeting dynamic.

jokes.text: Planet Express is on the move! For this hip, young delivery company, tomorrow is today... and today is yesterday! You heard me.
jokes.setup: Corporate promotional video with typical business buzzwords
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical time logic followed by defiant 'You heard me'
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate speak, the 'You heard me' adds defiant absurdity.

jokes.text: Watching myself work makes me hungry. Help me find food.
jokes.setup: Watching company promotional video
jokes.punchline: Random non-sequitur about being hungry from watching himself
jokes.explanation: Typical Professor non-sequitur, mild but fits character.

jokes.text: I'll not only find it, I'll help you do more to it.
jokes.setup: Professor asks for help finding food
jokes.punchline: Confusing offer to 'do more to' the food
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that doesn't quite land, unclear what it means.

jokes.text: A Bot-Mitzvah! Shalom, hunger. Shalom, free food.
jokes.setup: They arrive at some kind of robot religious ceremony
jokes.punchline: Bender's greeting combines Jewish tradition with his obsession with food
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment combining Jewish culture parody with his food obsession.

jokes.text: No shellfish! - That is so unfair. - Tell me about it.
jokes.setup: Kosher dietary restrictions at robot religious event
jokes.punchline: Characters complaining about religious dietary laws
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about religious restrictions, relatable complaint.

jokes.text: So you guys don't believe in Robot-Jesus? He was built and was well-programmed, but he wasn't our messiah.
jokes.setup: Discussion of robot religion
jokes.punchline: Robot Jesus described in computer terms - built and programmed instead of born
jokes.explanation: Clever religious parody adapting Christian concepts to robot terms.

jokes.text: It's been a terrible year. The company is on the verge of bankruptcy.
jokes.setup: Following upbeat corporate video about success
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction revealing company is actually failing
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke, classic contrast between PR and reality.

jokes.text: The blue pie slice represents the money we earned. The green slice represents an $8 bank error in our favor.
jokes.setup: Presenting financial charts
jokes.punchline: Revealing their 'earnings' include an $8 bank error
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how pathetic their finances are, specific detail works well.

jokes.text: I'll kill you! I own one share of Planet kajiggers, so I'm entitled to some answers.
jokes.setup: Angry about worthless stock
jokes.punchline: Death threat followed by modest claim of owning just one share
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg moment - extreme threat over minimal investment, fits his character.

jokes.text: Question: Why does no one visit me in my home?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg demanding answers as shareholder
jokes.punchline: His burning question is about his personal loneliness
jokes.explanation: Excellent Zoidberg moment - uses shareholder meeting for personal therapy, very in character.

jokes.text: Your apartment smells like Polygrip and cat pee!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg asks why no one visits
jokes.punchline: Brutal honest answer about his terrible living conditions
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Zoidberg's question, specific disgusting details work well.

jokes.text: This is perfect for me!
jokes.setup: Just heard about the terrible apartment conditions
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastically declares it perfect for him
jokes.explanation: Sets up That Guy as having low standards, good character establishment.

jokes.text: You didn't let me finish! I was going to say 'free food'!
jokes.setup: Asked about being cryogenically frozen for benefits
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was just asking about free food, not the freezing
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection, shows character's priorities are just free food.

jokes.text: When I was frozen, carrots ruled the earth. But now they don't. It takes some getting used to.
jokes.setup: Support group member sharing his adjustment difficulties
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim that carrots used to rule earth
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor, unexpected and memorable detail.

jokes.text: Bone-itis? That's a funny name for a horrible disease.
jokes.setup: That Guy mentions his terminal diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Fry comments on the silly-sounding name
jokes.explanation: Excellent made-up disease name that sounds both silly and medical, very quotable.

jokes.text: There was no cure. One drug company was close... but I arranged a hostile takeover and sold its assets. Made 100 mil.
jokes.setup: Explaining why there was no cure for his disease
jokes.punchline: He personally destroyed the company that could have cured him for profit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark irony - he caused his own doom through greed, perfect 80s businessman satire.

jokes.text: The hardest thing was seeing my wife on display in the British Museum.
jokes.setup: Caveman sharing his adjustment challenges
jokes.punchline: His wife is a museum exhibit as a prehistoric specimen
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever joke about the implications of being a frozen caveman.

jokes.text: That dance wasn't that safe.
jokes.setup: Asked about remembering 'Safety Dance'
jokes.punchline: Deadpan observation that the dance wasn't actually safe
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective observation about the song title vs. reality.

jokes.text: Awesome. Awesome to the max!
jokes.setup: Offered a potential job opportunity
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top 80s slang response
jokes.explanation: Good 80s character work, establishes his dated speech patterns.

jokes.text: Come on! I've got a lot of ransom notes to send!
jokes.setup: Told the post office meter is for business mail only
jokes.punchline: Bender's 'business' is sending ransom notes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - casual mention of criminal activity as normal business.

jokes.text: I move that everyone comes over... to snuggle my cat! - Second.
jokes.setup: Corporate meeting with formal motions
jokes.punchline: Motion is for everyone to snuggle his cat
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist corporate humor, mixing formal procedure with ridiculous content.

jokes.text: I move your cat stinks and is ugly! - Second.
jokes.setup: Following the cat-snuggling motion
jokes.punchline: Counter-motion insulting the cat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurd corporate meeting, direct insult as formal motion.

jokes.text: And my cat smells good and is pretty. - Second.
jokes.setup: Cat has been insulted in previous motion
jokes.punchline: Professor defends cat with equally formal counter-motion
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurd corporate cat debate, shows Professor's attachment.

jokes.text: I nominate that guy. Because he knows about business and stuff and has a tie.
jokes.setup: Need to nominate new CEO
jokes.punchline: Fry's criteria are 'business and stuff' and having a tie
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment showing his simple logic and poor judgment about business qualifications.

jokes.text: Shares still count if they were washed, right?
jokes.setup: About to vote shares in corporate election
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg thinks physical stock certificates work like clothing
jokes.explanation: Excellent Zoidberg moment showing his complete ignorance of finance and business.

jokes.text: Hold on! Scruffy votes his 40,000 shares for the stranger.
jokes.setup: Close vote between candidates
jokes.punchline: Janitor reveals he owns vastly more stock than anyone else
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - the janitor is secretly the biggest shareholder, perfect Scruffy moment.

jokes.text: Scruffy believes in this company.
jokes.setup: Asked why he has so much stock
jokes.punchline: Simple, earnest explanation for his massive investment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy line - deadpan, loyal, explains his secret investment with simple dignity.

jokes.text: And I'm voting against the cat-hater! Isn't that right, kitty?
jokes.setup: Tie-breaking vote needed, Professor appeals to his cat
jokes.punchline: References earlier cat insults and asks cat for approval
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the cat debate, shows Professor's priorities and attachment.

jokes.text: Hey! You ate my change!
jokes.setup: Professor consulting his cat about the vote
jokes.punchline: Cat apparently ate his coins, distracting from the important vote
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of cat eating money, distracts Professor at crucial moment.

jokes.text: Anyone who's a sheep is fired. Who's a sheep?
jokes.setup: New CEO giving motivational speech about sharks vs. sheep
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks people to identify as sheep so he can fire them
jokes.explanation: Good business satire - motivational speech as firing tactic, shows his manipulative nature.

jokes.text: There are two kinds of people: sheep and sharks. Anyone who's a sheep is fired. Who's a sheep?
jokes.setup: New CEO giving motivational business philosophy
jokes.punchline: Immediately threatens to fire people based on his metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good parody of aggressive business speak. The immediate threat to fire people based on a metaphor is absurd.

jokes.text: Excuse me, which one do people like to hug?
jokes.setup: CEO asks who's a sheep (to fire them)
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg misses the threat, asks which animal gets hugs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - completely misses the business threat, focuses on getting affection.

jokes.text: Gutsy question. You're a shark. Sharks don't look back... because they don't have necks. Necks are for sheep.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg's innocent question about hugging
jokes.punchline: Elaborate shark metaphor leading to 'necks are for sheep'
jokes.explanation: Excellent 80s business speak satire, anatomical logic is absurd but delivered with confidence.

jokes.text: Package delivery?! Oh, God!
jokes.setup: New CEO giving inspiring speech about leading the industry
jokes.punchline: Horrified to learn what industry he's actually in
jokes.explanation: Good business satire - CEO doesn't even know what the company does, shows 80s corporate excess.

jokes.text: Business plan? Yes. I keep it here, right next to my heart.
jokes.setup: Asked about his business plan
jokes.punchline: Pulls out what appears to be an escape plan instead
jokes.explanation: Good setup for visual gag, shows Professor's lack of real business planning.

jokes.text: This is no business plan, it's an escape plan. So long, suckers!
jokes.setup: Professor's 'business plan' revealed
jokes.punchline: It's actually an escape plan, Professor flees immediately
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to setup, Professor's cowardly escape fits character perfectly.

jokes.text: Fry, as a fellow '80s dollar jockey, I'm making you my new vice chairman.
jokes.setup: New CEO restructuring company
jokes.punchline: '80s dollar jockey' as business terminology
jokes.explanation: Good 80s slang, establishes bond between the two 80s characters.

jokes.text: That's what I call a hostile makeover. Hair gel?
jokes.setup: Just finished corporate takeover
jokes.punchline: 'Hostile makeover' pun combining business and beauty terms
jokes.explanation: Clever pun combining hostile takeover with makeover, fits 80s business culture.

jokes.text: No, thanks. I make my own.
jokes.setup: Offered hair gel
jokes.punchline: Fry's hair is apparently naturally greasy/styled
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about Fry's naturally greasy hair, fits character.

jokes.text: We'll shoot straight to the top and stay there, like Cindy Lauper!
jokes.setup: Motivational business speech
jokes.punchline: Uses Cyndi Lauper as example of sustained success
jokes.explanation: Good 80s reference showing character's dated perspective, Cyndi Lauper as business model.

jokes.text: We can't compete! Her company is big and evil. We're small and neutral.
jokes.setup: Discussing competition with Mom's company
jokes.punchline: Characterizes companies as big/evil vs small/neutral
jokes.explanation: Good business analysis in simple terms, sets up next joke about Switzerland/Germany.

jokes.text: Switzerland is small and neutral. We're Germany, ambitious and misunderstood.
jokes.setup: Bender says they're small and neutral
jokes.punchline: That Guy reframes them as Germany (with Nazi implications)
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - comparing company to Nazi Germany while calling it 'misunderstood' is brilliant satire.

jokes.text: Everyone wants to be like Germany. Do we have the strength of will?
jokes.setup: Just compared company to Germany
jokes.punchline: Asks if they have 'strength of will' (Nazi terminology)
jokes.explanation: Continues the dark Nazi parallel, 'strength of will' echoes fascist rhetoric.

jokes.text: Yes, my liege.
jokes.setup: That Guy asks if they'll let themselves be beaten
jokes.punchline: Bender submissively agrees they will lose
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment showing his lazy defeatism, medieval address adds humor.

jokes.text: Look at my butt!
jokes.setup: CEO challenging Mom to competition
jokes.punchline: Bender's contribution is mooning them
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender crude humor, fits character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You call that a pressed ham? Walt, hit the retaliate button!
jokes.setup: Bender shows his butt as insult
jokes.punchline: Mom critiques his mooning technique and escalates to violence
jokes.explanation: 'Pressed ham' is great slang for mooning, Mom's businesslike response to crude insult.

jokes.text: Any button! They all retaliate!
jokes.setup: Mom tells Walt to hit retaliate button
jokes.punchline: Reveals all her buttons are for retaliation/violence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Mom's evil nature, all options are violent.

jokes.text: Get your stinking trike off me, you damn, dirty ape!
jokes.setup: Watching gladiator-style ape fights
jokes.punchline: Planet of the Apes reference applied to ape tricycle combat
jokes.explanation: Good movie reference adapted to absurd context of ape tricycle fighting.

jokes.text: Work really hard? - No. - Oh, thank God!
jokes.setup: That Guy asks Fry to guess secret to success
jokes.punchline: Fry's relieved that hard work isn't the answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment showing his laziness, good setup/punchline structure.

jokes.text: It's time to update our company's image... and give it the sleek, dazzling veneer of the 1980s!
jokes.setup: Talking about updating company image
jokes.punchline: Wants to make it look like the 1980s (which is outdated)
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - 80s guy thinks 80s style is modern and sleek.

jokes.text: Delivery has nothing to do with our business.
jokes.setup: Told they haven't made deliveries since takeover
jokes.punchline: Claims delivery isn't relevant to delivery company
jokes.explanation: Excellent business satire - CEO dismisses company's core function for image.

jokes.text: I don't know what we do. Just kidding. What are we, a bus or something?
jokes.setup: Claims terrible ad won't let people know what they do
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't know either, guesses they're a bus
jokes.explanation: Great CEO incompetence joke - doesn't know what his own company does.

jokes.text: It grows the brand.
jokes.setup: Asked if he approved the terrible ad
jokes.punchline: Fry uses corporate buzzword 'grows the brand'
jokes.explanation: Good satire of Fry picking up corporate speak, buzzword as justification.

jokes.text: Sold!
jokes.setup: Amy says she'd sell her stock for a sandwich
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg immediately agrees to buy it
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg moment showing his poor financial judgment, excellent economy.

jokes.text: You got fleeced. I would have settled for a hard roll with ketchup inside.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg bought Amy's stock for a sandwich
jokes.punchline: Bender reveals Amy could have gotten much less
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how worthless the stock really is, disgusting 'sandwich' description.

jokes.text: I'm sick of hearing about those turtle squirts!
jokes.setup: Angry about Planet Express getting attention as 'New Delivery Kings'
jokes.punchline: 'Turtle squirts' as dismissive insult
jokes.explanation: Good Mom insult combining slow (turtle) and weak (squirt) imagery.

jokes.text: Jam a bastard in it, you crap!
jokes.setup: Son tries to correct her about the 'kings' thing
jokes.punchline: Mom's bizarre angry outburst with nonsensical grammar
jokes.explanation: Classic Mom line - completely nonsensical but delivered with conviction, very quotable.

jokes.text: Don't worry. Let me worry about 'blank.'
jokes.setup: That Guy teaching him executive speak about being worried about 'blank'
jokes.punchline: Fry literally says the word 'blank'
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor - Fry takes the template literally and says 'blank'.

jokes.text: Good. I also would have accepted: 'Blank? Blank! You're not looking at the big picture!'
jokes.setup: Approving Fry's use of the word 'blank'
jokes.punchline: Alternative responses also using the literal word 'blank'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the meta joke, more executive speak templates with 'blank'.

jokes.text: You get back to the farm, shift some paradigms.
jokes.setup: Dismissing Fry during business confrontation
jokes.punchline: 'Shift some paradigms' as meaningless business task
jokes.explanation: Good business speak satire, 'paradigm shifting' as busywork.

jokes.text: I'll handle this, Fry. You get back to the farm, shift some paradigms.
jokes.setup: Confrontation with Planet Express crew
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical corporate task mixing farm work with buzzwords
jokes.explanation: Good absurd mixing of agricultural and corporate metaphors

jokes.text: Call me a booger-blaster! I'll blast a booger...
jokes.setup: Leela called That Guy a booger-blaster
jokes.punchline: Fry takes the insult literally and threatens to blast boogers
jokes.explanation: Mild Fry moment taking insult literally, somewhat juvenile humor.

jokes.text: That Guy is a con man, and you've been hypnotized by his bologna!
jokes.setup: Leela warning about That Guy being deceptive
jokes.punchline: 'Hypnotized by his bologna' as description of being fooled
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay using 'bologna' (nonsense) in hypnosis context.

jokes.text: Suz, have I changed? - No, Mr. Fry. - Thanks, doll.
jokes.setup: Fry asking if he's changed after becoming executive
jokes.punchline: Asks his secretary who gives him the answer he wants
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Fry asking subordinate if he's changed, calling her 'doll' shows he has.

jokes.text: Right. We're not a traditional family like the Murphys... or the lesbian coven, but we're a family.
jokes.setup: Talking about Planet Express being like a family
jokes.punchline: Compares traditional family to lesbian coven as equal examples
jokes.explanation: Good future society humor treating lesbian coven as normal family type.

jokes.text: I don't want ass prints on my new door!
jokes.setup: Telling people not to let door hit them on way out
jokes.punchline: Worried about literal ass prints on his door
jokes.explanation: Takes common expression literally, shows That Guy's vanity about his new office.

jokes.text: I had no idea the company would be sold! I was just an innocent suck-up!
jokes.setup: Fry realizing the consequences of the takeover
jokes.punchline: Admits to being a 'suck-up' while claiming innocence
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment combining self-awareness with self-justification.

jokes.text: I don't know. I'm a program in the intercom.
jokes.setup: Fry asks if he's a good person
jokes.punchline: Secretary reveals she's just an AI program
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that the secretary was AI all along, deadpan delivery perfect.

jokes.text: I have more important things to do today than laugh and clap my hands.
jokes.setup: Told his 2:00 magician appointment is here
jokes.punchline: Claims magician show is beneath him now
jokes.explanation: Shows Fry's transformation into pompous executive, good character development.

jokes.text: I'll vote it down like a raise for school teachers!
jokes.setup: Promising to vote against takeover
jokes.punchline: Compares to voting against teacher raises with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about petty civic meanness with perfect delivery

jokes.text: Zoidberg owned 51% of the company? - The shares were worthless! He asked for toilet paper!
jokes.setup: Shocked that Zoidberg had majority control
jokes.punchline: Professor reveals he gave Zoidberg worthless shares for toilet paper
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - worthless shares for toilet paper, explains Zoidberg's majority stake.

jokes.text: The ballot was confusing!
jokes.setup: Mom's shareholders voted against their own interests
jokes.punchline: Blames confusing ballot (like 2000 Florida election)
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to election ballot confusion, decent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I'm a millionaire! Suddenly I have an opinion about the capital gains tax!
jokes.setup: Just became rich from stock sale
jokes.punchline: Immediately develops political opinions about rich people's taxes
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of how money instantly changes political views, very quotable and insightful.

jokes.text: Wait! My sandwich! Has it also appreciated in value? Please!
jokes.setup: Everyone's stocks are worth $107 each
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg desperately hopes his sandwich investment paid off too
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment, pathetic hope about sandwich investment, good callback.

jokes.text: You didn't refrigerate it, spineless! - You had to drag spines into this!
jokes.setup: Amy calls Zoidberg spineless about the sandwich
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg takes 'spineless' literally as anatomical insult
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg moment taking insult literally due to his anatomy, clever wordplay.

jokes.text: I'm an '80s guy. Friendship means for $2... I beat you till you got detached retinas.
jokes.setup: Fry appeals to their friendship
jokes.punchline: That Guy's warped 80s view of friendship involves violence for money
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark 80s satire - friendship as paid violence, specific medical detail adds impact.

jokes.text: My bones! Oh, my God! His bone-itis!
jokes.setup: That Guy suddenly grabs his bones in pain
jokes.punchline: The bone-itis from earlier is finally killing him
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback payoff - the disease mentioned earlier becomes his downfall.

jokes.text: I was so busy being an '80s guy, I forgot to cure it!
jokes.setup: Dying of bone-itis
jokes.punchline: Admits he was too focused on 80s persona to get medical treatment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - died because he prioritized image over health, great irony.

jokes.text: My only regret is... that I have... bone-itis!
jokes.setup: That Guy's dying words
jokes.punchline: His only regret is having the silly-named disease
jokes.explanation: Legendary Futurama quote - perfect dying words callback to the silly disease name, became cultural touchstone.

jokes.text: I'll be whatever I want to do!
jokes.setup: Mom calls him a fool
jokes.punchline: Fry's defiant but grammatically confused response
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry language confusion, mixing 'be whatever I want' with 'do whatever I want'.

jokes.text: Millionaires nothing! The stock's only worth three kajiggers!
jokes.setup: Everyone excited about being millionaires
jokes.punchline: Stock crashed to worthless 'kajiggers' again
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of the wealth fantasy, callback to made-up currency name.

jokes.text: Once again, the conservative sandwich-heavy portfolio... pays off for the hungry investor!
jokes.setup: Everyone else lost money in the market crash
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's sandwich 'investment' is the only one that holds value
jokes.explanation: Brilliant financial satire - treating sandwich as investment portfolio, perfect Zoidberg victory moment, highly quotable.

tmdb_overview: With Planet Express in financial trouble, Fry nominates a flashy businessman from the 1980s to replace Professor Farnsworth as CEO of the company.
